125	// DoltDB wraps access to the underlying noms database and hides some of the details of the underlying storage.
126	type DoltDB struct {
127		db  hooksDatabase
128		vrw types.ValueReadWriter
129		ns  tree.NodeStore
130	
131		// databaseName holds the name of the database for this DoltDB instance. Note that this name may not be
132		// populated for all DoltDB instances. For filesystem based databases, the database name is determined
133		// by looking through the filepath in reverse, finding the first .dolt directory, and then taking the
134		// parent directory as the database name. For non-filesystem based databases, the database name will not
135		// currently be populated.
136		databaseName string
137	
138		// Keep a LRU Cache of materialized commits to speed up future commit resolutions
139		commitCache *lru.Cache[hash.Hash, *OptionalCommit]
140	}
141	
142	// IsWorkingSetRef reports whether |ref| identifies the working set or staging area rather than a commit.
143	func IsWorkingSetRef(ref string) bool {
144		return ref == Working || ref == Staged
145	}
146	
147	// DoltDBFromCS creates a DoltDB from a noms chunks.ChunkStore
148	func DoltDBFromCS(cs chunks.ChunkStore, databaseName string) (*DoltDB, error) {
149		vrw := types.NewValueStore(cs)
150		ns := tree.NewNodeStore(cs)
151		db := datas.NewTypesDatabase(vrw, ns)
152		commitCache, err := lru.New[hash.Hash, *OptionalCommit](commitCacheSize)
153		if err != nil {
154			return nil, err
155		}
156		ret := &DoltDB{
157			db:           hooksDatabase{Database: db},
158			vrw:          vrw,
159			ns:           ns,
160			databaseName: databaseName,
161			commitCache:  commitCache,
162		}
163		ret.db.db = ret
164		return ret, nil
165	}
166	
167	// GetDatabaseName returns the name of the database.
168	// Note: This can return an empty string if the database name is not populated.
169	func (ddb *DoltDB) GetDatabaseName() string {
170		return ddb.databaseName
171	}

174	// This method should really only be used in cases where we need to get to the internals of the database, such
175	// as scanning for corruption or migrating data formats. Generally avoid this function.
176	func ExposeDatabaseFromDoltDB(ddb *DoltDB) datas.Database {
177		return ddb.db

34	type CommitHook interface {
35		// Execute is arbitrary read-only function whose arguments are new Dataset commit into a specific Database.
36		// The returned values are a callback function and an error. The callback function is
37		// a Wait function which is optional. If returned, it will be registered with the ReplicationStatusController. If the
38		// hook implements NotifyWaitFailedCommitHook, it will be notified if the Wait function returns an error.
39		// The |Error| returned is actually ignored by the caller. It exists primarily for for unit testing. Any error which
40		// happens in a hook should be logged by the hook itself.

## How to use this / caveats
- **Highest-risk long-lived retainers** (fix these first in a rework): the dbfactory `singletons` map (§A), `DoltDatabaseProvider.databases`/`gitRemotes` (§D), `DoltEnv.doltDB` (§C), session `branchState.dbData` + `DatabaseCache`/`GlobalState` (§E), the CLI `Queryist` cache (§F), and the remotesrv/cscache chunk-store caches (§G). These outlive a single query and will hand out a stale handle after a swap.
- **The interface-typed (⚠) holders are the trap** the ticket warned about — they retain a storage layer through `Database`/`ChunkStore`/`rootTracker`/`SqlDatabase`/`CommitItr`/`RootSetter`/`ClusterStatusProvider`/`GRPCDialProvider`/`RemoteSrvStore` and never mention `DoltDB`, so a name-based grep would miss them entirely.
- Tests/benchmarks were excluded (e.g. `gc_test.go` `purgingSafepointController`, `testcommands.MultiRepoTestSetup`, `chunks.TestStoreView`, nbs `benchmarks/*`, `DatabaseSuite`).
- Coverage caveat from the index: static analysis does not trace reflection, `init()`-registered handlers, or function-value fields. `env.actions/*`, `dprocedures/*`, `merge`, `revert`, `rebase` etc. *call* `DoltDB` methods but pass it as an argument rather than retaining it in a field — they are not stale-handle risks and are omitted. Spot-check the singleton cache and provider maps by hand before the rework, since those are the two places a swap must invalidate.
